libraries.description: SMIP JS SDK - generic, tenant-agnostic apiDemoMethods helpers + descriptors. Two mutations (updateAttribute, updateObject) and three queries (getTypes, getObject, getEnumTypes) wrap the common SMIP / PostGraphile surface. Common code; tenant-agnostic. See README.md for full docs.

script_templates.script:
'auto', 'relative' => false, 'detectDebug' => false)); // HTMLHelper::_('script', "$primary_domain/media/com_thinkiq/js/dist/tiq.tiqGraphQL.js", array('version' => 'auto', 'relative' => false, 'detectDebug' => false)); // HTMLHelper::_('script', "$primary_domain/media/com_thinkiq/js/dist/tiq.components.js", array('version' => 'auto', 'relative' => false, 'detectDebug' => false)); // HTMLHelper::_('script', "$primary_domain/media/com_thinkiq/js/dist/tiq.charts.js", array('version' => 'auto', 'relative' => false, 'detectDebug' => false)); require_once 'thinkiq_context.php'; $context = new Context(); $php_api = new TiqUtilities\Model\Script('smip_js_sdk.api_template'); $php_api_file_name = $php_api->script_file_name; // Pulls in the descriptor catalog (apiDemoTools[]) — see smip_js_sdk.api_tools. // SMIP JS SDK contributes generic mutation helpers via apiDemoMethods but // adds NO descriptor entries; this page renders an empty tool list unless // a tenant-specific library has also been included before it, in which // case those descriptors show up. TiqUtilities\Model\Script::includeScript('smip_js_sdk.api_tools'); ?>

{{pageTitle}} source

SMIP JS SDK contributes generic mutation helpers (apiDemoMethods.updateAttribute, apiDemoMethods.updateObject) and query helpers (apiDemoMethods.getTypes, apiDemoMethods.getObject, apiDemoMethods.getEnumTypes) but adds no descriptor entries to apiDemoTools. Include a tenant-specific tenant-specific library on top of this one to populate this documentation page.

<?php

// Purpose: this script is a generic PHP API dispatch skeleton for any
// SMIP-side script that wants to expose server-side functions to the
// browser (or to other scripts) via tiqJSHelper.invokeScriptAsync().
//
// The pattern is a simple switch on $context->std_inputs->method:
//   - $f          — the requested function name (e.g. "MyTenantMethod")
//   - $a          — the parsed arguments object (from JSON in std_inputs->data)
//   - JsonResponse — Joomla's JSON envelope used to return values to the caller
//
// Tenant-specific libraries that want their own PHP-side methods should
// either fork this template into their own api_template script, or
// `includeScript` it and add `case "<MethodName>":` blocks to the switch
// below. The skeleton here ships with no cases — SMIP JS SDK is JS-only
// (mutations + getTypes); PHP cases are the tenant's to fill in.

require_once 'thinkiq_context.php';
$context = new Context();

TiqUtilities\Model\Script::includeScript('smip_js_sdk.guzzle_client');

use Joomla\CMS\Response\JsonResponse; // Used for returning data to the client.
if (!defined('JPATH_BASE')) define('JPATH_BASE', dirname(__DIR__));

$f = isset($context->std_inputs->method) ? $context->std_inputs->method : '';
$a = isset($context->std_inputs->data)   ? json_decode($context->std_inputs->data) : '';

use TiqUtilities\GraphQL\GraphQL;

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------
// Tenant-specific PHP-side functions go here. Define them above and
// add a matching `case "<MethodName>":` block to the switch below.
//
// Example (commented out — copy & adjust):
//
// function MyTenantMethod($someArg){
//     $client = new Guzzler();
//     return $client->GetAsync("some/path/$someArg");
// }
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------

switch ($f){
    // case "MyTenantMethod":
    //     $returnObject = MyTenantMethod($a->someArg);
    //     die(new JsonResponse($returnObject));
    //     break;
}

<script>
//
// SMIP JS SDK — generic, tenant-agnostic apiDemoMethods helpers + a
// minimal apiDemoTools catalog of generic queries and mutations.
//
// What this library carries:
//   • apiDemoMethods._buildPatchLiteral(patch)            — patch builder
//   • apiDemoMethods.updateAttribute({ attributeId, patch }) — generic mutation
//   • apiDemoMethods.updateObject({ id, patch })         — generic mutation
//   • apiDemoMethods.getTypes(args)                       — generic tiqTypes query
//   • apiDemoMethods.getObject(args)                      — generic single-object fetch
//   • apiDemoMethods.getEnumTypes(args)                   — enumerationTypes + zipped value objects
//
// Plus matching apiDemoTools descriptors so the doc page renders an
// interactive form for each one. The mutation forms are dangerous —
// a wrong attribute id and a `null` patch silently clears that
// column; treat the doc page as a developer-only debug surface.
//
// IDs are BigInt on the server. PostGraphile / GraphQL encode BigInt as
// quoted JSON strings to dodge JavaScript's Number 2^53 cliff. Every id
// field in this SDK — node ids, attributeId, referencedNodeId, intValue —
// is a digits-only string both inbound (callers must pass strings) and
// outbound (responses come back as strings). The /^\d+$/ guards on the
// handlers enforce this on the way in.
//
// Tenant-specific libraries include this SDK first, then add their own
// descriptors and handler methods on top of the same `apiDemoTools` /
// `apiDemoMethods` globals. The merge is additive — both libraries use
// the `(typeof X !== 'undefined') ? X : default` guard so load order
// doesn't matter:
//
//     var apiDemoTools   = (typeof apiDemoTools   !== 'undefined') ? apiDemoTools   : [];
//     var apiDemoMethods = (typeof apiDemoMethods !== 'undefined') ? apiDemoMethods : {};
//     Object.assign(apiDemoMethods, { /* this library's methods */ });
//
// To consume both layers from a display script:
//
//     <?php
//     TiqUtilities\Model\Script::includeScript('smip_js_sdk.api_tools');
//     TiqUtilities\Model\Script::includeScript('your_tenant_js_sdk.api_tools');
//     ?>
//

// Additive globals — load order doesn't matter; later loads merge in.
var apiDemoTools   = (typeof apiDemoTools   !== 'undefined') ? apiDemoTools   : [];
var apiDemoMethods = (typeof apiDemoMethods !== 'undefined') ? apiDemoMethods : {};


// =====================================================================
// Descriptor catalog — what shows up in the doc page
// =====================================================================

apiDemoTools.push(

    //
    // Mutation — UpdateAttribute. ⚠ DESTRUCTIVE: writes the supplied
    // patch fields onto an existing attribute. Pass attributeId and a
    // patch object — one value field per ScalarTypeEnum datatype:
    //   BOOL → boolValue, INT → intValue, FLOAT → floatValue,
    //   STRING → stringValue, DATETIME → datetimeValue,
    //   INTERVAL → intervalValue (IntervalInput object),
    //   OBJECT → objectValue (stringified JSON),
    //   ENUMERATION → enumerationValue (+ companion enumerationTypeId /
    //     enumerationValues / enumerationName),
    //   GEOPOINT → geopointValue, REFERENCE → referencedNodeId.
    // Plus the meta `dataType` field (ScalarTypeEnum) to re-type the
    // attribute. Any field set to null clears the column. Multiple
    // patch keys are allowed. See the apiDemoMethods.updateAttribute
    // header below for the full per-field example block.
    //
    {
        key:         "updateAttributeViaJs",
        name:        "UpdateAttributeViaJs",
        category:    "Mutation (JS)",
        description: "Update an attribute's value(s) by id. DESTRUCTIVE — writes to the SoR. attributeId is a digits-only attribute node id. patch is a JS object with one or more AttributePatch fields, one per ScalarTypeEnum datatype: boolValue (BOOL), intValue (INT, BigInt — string-encode), floatValue (FLOAT), stringValue (STRING), datetimeValue (DATETIME, ISO-8601), intervalValue (INTERVAL, IntervalInput object), objectValue (OBJECT, stringified JSON), enumerationValue (ENUMERATION), geopointValue (GEOPOINT), referencedNodeId (REFERENCE, BigInt FK). Companion ENUMERATION wiring fields (enumerationTypeId / enumerationValues / enumerationName) and the meta `dataType` field are also on the patch. Pass `null` for any field to clear that column to GraphQL null; omit a field (or use `undefined` in code) to skip it. The handler is apiDemoMethods.updateAttribute, which lives here in this library.",
        via:         "js",
        handler:     "updateAttribute",
        inputs: [
            { key: "attributeId", argName: "attributeId", label: "Attribute id (digits): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "patch", argName: "patch", label: "Patch (JSON object): ", type: "text",
              default: '{"floatValue": 0}',
              parse: "json", showJsonPreview: true }
        ],
        output: { kind: "single" }
    },

    //
    // Mutation — UpdateObject. ⚠ DESTRUCTIVE: writes the supplied
    // patch fields onto an existing object. Common patches:
    //   { "importance": -260420 }   — sort key (lower = first)
    //   { "displayName": "..." }    — rename
    //   { "description": "..." }    — set/clear description
    //
    {
        key:         "updateObjectViaJs",
        name:        "UpdateObjectViaJs",
        category:    "Mutation (JS)",
        description: "Update an object's properties by id. DESTRUCTIVE — writes to the SoR. id is a digits-only object node id. patch is a JS object with one or more ObjectPatch fields: importance (int), displayName (string), description (string). Pass `null` for a field to clear it; omit a field to skip it. Common at TAF Carbon for setting `importance: -<yymmdd>` so newest runs sort to the top of instance lists.",
        via:         "js",
        handler:     "updateObject",
        inputs: [
            { key: "id", argName: "id", label: "Object id (digits): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "patch", argName: "patch", label: "Patch (JSON object): ", type: "text",
              default: '{"importance": 0}',
              parse: "json", showJsonPreview: true }
        ],
        output: { kind: "single" }
    },

    //
    // Query — GetTypes. List tiqTypes with optional filters and
    // optional related-data subselections. Each include-* toggle adds
    // a fragment to the GraphQL query (more data = more cost), so the
    // default-OFF posture keeps the round-trip lean for "just show me
    // the types" use cases. Two search strings (typeNameSearch +
    // instanceNameSearch) push case-insensitive substring filters
    // server-side via PostGraphile's includesInsensitive.
    //
    // Use this to:
    //   * Enumerate a tenant's type system (typeNameSearch='').
    //   * Find a type's attribute schema before spawning instances
    //     (typeNameSearch='Lignin Lot', includeTypeAttributes=true).
    //   * List instances of a type ('LA Storage', includeInstances=true).
    //   * Scope to a sample by name ('LA Storage', includeInstances=true,
    //     instanceNameSearch='LA-260424', includeInstanceAttributes=true).
    //   * Walk supertype/subtype/composition relationships.
    //
    {
        key:         "getTypesViaJs",
        name:        "GetTypesViaJs",
        category:    "Query (JS)",
        description: "List tiqTypes with optional filters and optional related-data subselections. typeNameSearch filters types by case-insensitive displayName substring; empty input returns every type. The 6 toggles add subselections — each is more data over the wire, defaults all OFF for a lean response. Use this to enumerate a tenant's type system, find a type's attribute schema before spawning instances (set includeTypeAttributes), list instances of a type (set includeInstances + optional instanceNameSearch + optional includeInstanceAttributes), or walk supertype / subtype / composition. Returns the raw tiqTypes list with whatever subselections are turned on. The shape is intentionally not flattened — this is an exploration tool, not a row-builder.",
        via:         "js",
        handler:     "getTypes",
        inputs: [
            { key: "typeNameSearch", argName: "typeNameSearch", label: "Type name search (substring, optional): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "includeTypeAttributes", argName: "includeTypeAttributes", label: "Include type attributes (typeToAttributeTypes)? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeInstances", argName: "includeInstances", label: "Include instances (objectsByTypeId)? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "instanceNameSearch", argName: "instanceNameSearch", label: "Instance name search (substring, optional, only when instances on): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "includeInstanceAttributes", argName: "includeInstanceAttributes", label: "Include attributes on each instance? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeSubTypes", argName: "includeSubTypes", label: "Include subtypes? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeSuperType", argName: "includeSuperType", label: "Include supertype (subTypeOf)? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeComposition", argName: "includeComposition", label: "Include composition (childEquipment)? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false }
        ],
        output: { kind: "array" }
    },

    //
    // Query — GetObject. Fetch a single object by id with optional
    // related-data subselections. The everyday workhorse for type-bound
    // display scripts that need to load their own instance — pass
    // context.std_inputs.node_id and pick which slices of the graph
    // come along: attributes (default ON, all ten ScalarTypeEnum value
    // variants + dataType + dataSource + importance + relativeName),
    // parent (partOf), children (childObjects, optionally with their
    // own attributes folded in), and type (a small
    // {id displayName relativeName} pointer). Mirrors the Python
    // pattern of "get this object plus a couple of related rows in
    // one round-trip".
    //
    {
        key:         "getObjectViaJs",
        name:        "GetObjectViaJs",
        category:    "Query (JS)",
        description: "Fetch a single object by id with optional related-data subselections. nodeId is a digits-only SMIP object node id (REQUIRED). The 5 toggles control what comes back alongside the object's identity fields (id, displayName, relativeName, description, typeId, typeName, partOfId, importance). includeAttributes (default ON) returns ALL TEN attribute value-variants — boolValue (BOOL) / intValue (INT) / floatValue (FLOAT) / stringValue (STRING) / datetimeValue (DATETIME) / intervalValue (INTERVAL — { seconds, minutes, hours, days, months, years }) / objectValue (OBJECT) / enumerationValue (ENUMERATION) / geopointValue (GEOPOINT) / referencedNodeId (REFERENCE) — plus the attribute's dataType (ScalarTypeEnum), dataSource, importance, enumerationName for human-readable enum labels, and relativeName. The caller routes on attribute.dataType to pick the matching value field without a second query. includePartOf adds the parent object (id / displayName / relativeName / typeId / typeName). includeChildObjects adds direct children (id / displayName / relativeName / description / typeId / typeName / importance). includeChildAttributes folds the same attributes fragment into each child — only effective when includeChildObjects is also on. includeType adds a small type pointer. Returns the object dict with whatever subselections are turned on, or null if no object with that id was found. Use this in any type-bound display script that needs to load its own node.",
        via:         "js",
        handler:     "getObject",
        inputs: [
            { key: "nodeId", argName: "nodeId", label: "Node id (digits): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "includeAttributes", argName: "includeAttributes", label: "Include attributes? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: true },
            { key: "includePartOf", argName: "includePartOf", label: "Include partOf (parent object)? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeChildObjects", argName: "includeChildObjects", label: "Include childObjects? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeChildAttributes", argName: "includeChildAttributes", label: "Include children's attributes? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false },
            { key: "includeType", argName: "includeType", label: "Include type pointer? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: false }
        ],
        output: { kind: "single" }
    },

    //
    // Query — GetEnumTypes. List enumerationTypes (optionally filtered by
    // displayName substring) and zip the four parallel arrays the SMIP
    // stores (enumerationNames / defaultEnumerationValues /
    // enumerationColorCodes / enumerationDescriptions) into a single
    // `enumerationTypeObjects` array of {name, value, color, description}
    // objects. The flat arrays stay on the row for callers that need
    // positional access; the zipped objects are the friendlier shape for
    // rendering / lookup.
    //
    {
        key:         "getEnumTypesViaJs",
        name:        "GetEnumTypesViaJs",
        category:    "Query (JS)",
        description: "List enumerationTypes with optional displayName filter and an optional zip step. displayNameSearch filters by case-insensitive substring; empty returns every enumeration type. When includeValues is on (default), each result row gets an extra `enumerationTypeObjects` array of { name, value, color, description } objects, assembled by zipping the four parallel arrays the SMIP stores (enumerationNames / defaultEnumerationValues / enumerationColorCodes / enumerationDescriptions). The flat arrays stay on the row for positional access. Returns the raw enumerationTypes array.",
        via:         "js",
        handler:     "getEnumTypes",
        inputs: [
            { key: "displayNameSearch", argName: "displayNameSearch", label: "Enum type name search (substring, optional): ", type: "text",
              default: '',
              parse: "raw" },
            { key: "includeValues", argName: "includeValues", label: "Include zipped enumerationTypeObjects? ", type: "checkbox",
              default: true }
        ],
        output: { kind: "array" }
    }

);


// =====================================================================
// apiDemoMethods — the two mutation helpers (always direct-callable,
// also handlers for the two Mutation descriptors above) plus the
// getTypes / getObject / getEnumTypes query handlers.
// =====================================================================

Object.assign(apiDemoMethods, {

    // Build a GraphQL `patch: { ... }` literal from a JS object.
    //
    // Distinguishes three states per field:
    //   • undefined  — skip entirely (field doesn't appear in patch).
    //   • null       — emit `field: null` (clears the column to GraphQL null).
    //   • everything else — JSON-encode and emit as the value.
    //
    // Numbers are emitted bare (so float values arrive as floats, not
    // string literals); strings get JSON.stringify quoting; booleans
    // emit `true` / `false`. This is the same semantics the Python
    // `update_attribute` uses with its `_UNSET` sentinel for skip vs.
    // explicit-null. Returns the rendered `{ ... }` literal as a string,
    // ready to interpolate into a mutation body.
    _buildPatchLiteral: function(patch){
        const parts = [];
        for (const key of Object.keys(patch || {})) {
            const v = patch[key];
            if (v === undefined) continue;             // skip
            if (v === null)        { parts.push(`${key}: null`); continue; }
            if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') {
                parts.push(`${key}: ${v}`);
            } else {
                // String, or anything else — round-trip through JSON.stringify
                // so embedded quotes / backslashes are properly escaped.
                parts.push(`${key}: ${JSON.stringify(String(v))}`);
            }
        }
        return `{ ${parts.join(', ')} }`;
    },

    // Update an attribute's value(s) by id.
    //
    // Args shape:
    //   { attributeId, patch }
    //     attributeId — digits-only attribute node id (REQUIRED).
    //     patch       — plain JS object of fields to set on AttributePatch.
    //                   Value fields — one per ScalarTypeEnum datatype:
    //                     { boolValue:        true }                            // BOOL
    //                     { intValue:         "42" }                            // INT      (BigInt — string-encode large ints)
    //                     { floatValue:       12.5 }                            // FLOAT
    //                     { stringValue:      "abc" }                           // STRING
    //                     { datetimeValue:    "2026-04-20T10:00:00Z" }          // DATETIME (ISO-8601)
    //                     { intervalValue:    { ... IntervalInput ... } }       // INTERVAL (input object, not scalar)
    //                     { objectValue:      '{"x":1}' }                       // OBJECT   (stringified JSON)
    //                     { enumerationValue: "1" }                             // ENUMERATION (the selected enum value)
    //                     { geopointValue:    "..." }                           // GEOPOINT (schema types this as String)
    //                     { referencedNodeId: "1234" }                          // REFERENCE (BigInt FK to another node)
    //
    //                   Any of the above set to `null` clears the column to GraphQL null:
    //                     { datetimeValue:    null }
    //                     { objectValue:      null }
    //                     { intervalValue:    null }
    //                     { referencedNodeId: null }
    //
    //                   ENUMERATION wiring (companion fields, also clear to null):
    //                     { enumerationTypeId: "5" }     // FK to enumeration type
    //                     { enumerationValues: [...] }   // candidate set ([String])
    //                     { enumerationName:   "On" }    // human label for chosen value
    //
    //                   Meta:
    //                     { dataType: "FLOAT" }          // re-types the attribute (ScalarTypeEnum)
    //
    //                   Multiple patch keys are allowed; each maps to
    //                   one field on the AttributePatch input. Fields
    //                   set to `undefined` are skipped, `null` clears
    //                   the column. At least one key must be present.
    //                   The companion fields (enumerationName / enumerationValues /
    //                   enumerationTypeId) and the meta `dataType` field are also
    //                   on AttributePatch and follow the same skip/null rules.
    //
    // Returns the GraphQL response (full envelope), so callers can
    // inspect `data.updateAttribute.clientMutationId` if they want.
    updateAttribute: async function(args){
        const attributeId = String((args && args.attributeId) || '').trim();
        const patch       = (args && args.patch) || {};
        if (!attributeId || !/^\d+$/.test(attributeId)) {
            throw new Error('updateAttribute: attributeId must be digits-only; got ' + JSON.stringify(attributeId));
        }
        if (Object.keys(patch).length === 0) {
            throw new Error('updateAttribute: patch must contain at least one field');
        }
        const patchLit = apiDemoMethods._buildPatchLiteral(patch);
        const mutation = `
            mutation UpdateAttribute {
              updateAttribute(input: {
                id: "${attributeId}",
                patch: ${patchLit}
              }) { clientMutationId }
            }
        `;
        return await tiqJSHelper.invokeGraphQLAsync(mutation);
    },

    // Update an object's properties (importance, displayName, description, …).
    //
    // Args shape:
    //   { id, patch }
    //     id    — digits-only object node id (REQUIRED).
    //     patch — plain JS object of fields to set on the ObjectPatch input:
    //               { importance: -260420 }
    //               { displayName: "RL-260420-1" }
    //               { description: "...", importance: 5 }
    //             Same null/undefined semantics as updateAttribute's patch.
    //
    // Returns the GraphQL response envelope.
    updateObject: async function(args){
        const id    = String((args && args.id) || '').trim();
        const patch = (args && args.patch) || {};
        if (!id || !/^\d+$/.test(id)) {
            throw new Error('updateObject: id must be digits-only; got ' + JSON.stringify(id));
        }
        if (Object.keys(patch).length === 0) {
            throw new Error('updateObject: patch must contain at least one field');
        }
        const patchLit = apiDemoMethods._buildPatchLiteral(patch);
        const mutation = `
            mutation UpdateObject {
              updateObject(input: {
                id: "${id}",
                patch: ${patchLit}
              }) {
                object { id importance displayName description }
              }
            }
        `;
        return await tiqJSHelper.invokeGraphQLAsync(mutation);
    },

    // Query — list tiqTypes with optional filters and optional
    // related-data subselections. Mirrors the Python automation
    // helpers `get_type_by_display_name` (single-type lookup) plus
    // some of the patterns we'd otherwise write by hand inline:
    // listing types, finding a type's attribute schema, listing
    // instances under a type, walking sub/super-type relationships.
    //
    // Args shape (all optional except as noted):
    //   {
    //     typeNameSearch:            String — case-insensitive substring on tiqType.displayName
    //                                         (PostGraphile `includesInsensitive`). Empty = all types.
    //     includeTypeAttributes:     Boolean — add `typeToAttributeTypes { id displayName relativeName dataType sourceCategory importance }`.
    //     includeInstances:          Boolean — add `objectsByTypeId(filter: ...) { id displayName relativeName description typeId typeName }`.
    //     instanceNameSearch:        String — case-insensitive substring on instance.displayName,
    //                                         only used when includeInstances is true.
    //     includeInstanceAttributes: Boolean — add `attributes { ... }` to each instance,
    //                                         only used when includeInstances is true.
    //     includeSubTypes:           Boolean — add `subTypes { id displayName relativeName }`.
    //     includeSuperType:          Boolean — add `subTypeOf { id displayName relativeName }`.
    //     includeComposition:        Boolean — add `childEquipment { id displayName }`.
    //                                         Best-effort field shape; refine if the schema differs.
    //   }
    //
    // Returns the raw `tiqTypes` array from the GraphQL response.
    // Each entry is `{ id, displayName, relativeName, description,
    // fqn, idPath }` plus whatever optional subselections were turned
    // on. Intentionally not flattened — this is an exploration tool.
    getTypes: async function(args){
        args = args || {};
        const typeNameSearch            = String(args.typeNameSearch     || '').trim();
        const includeTypeAttributes     = !!args.includeTypeAttributes;
        const includeInstances          = !!args.includeInstances;
        const instanceNameSearch        = String(args.instanceNameSearch || '').trim();
        const includeInstanceAttributes = !!args.includeInstanceAttributes;
        const includeSubTypes           = !!args.includeSubTypes;
        const includeSuperType          = !!args.includeSuperType;
        const includeComposition        = !!args.includeComposition;

        // Optional case-insensitive filter on the outer tiqTypes — pushed
        // down server-side. Omitted entirely when empty so all types come
        // back without a redundant predicate.
        const typeFilter = typeNameSearch
            ? `(filter: { displayName: { includesInsensitive: ${JSON.stringify(typeNameSearch)} } })`
            : '';

        // Inner instance filter — only used when includeInstances is on.
        const instFilter = (includeInstances && instanceNameSearch)
            ? `(filter: { displayName: { includesInsensitive: ${JSON.stringify(instanceNameSearch)} } })`
            : '';

        // Each subselection is its own conditional fragment, spliced into
        // the query body if its toggle is on. Empty string when off — so
        // the rendered query stays as small as the caller asked for.
        const typeAttrSel = includeTypeAttributes
            ? `typeToAttributeTypes {
                    id displayName relativeName dataType sourceCategory importance
                  }`
            : '';

        // Mirror the getObject attribute fragment: ALL TEN value fields
        // plus dataType, so the caller can route on attribute.dataType
        // (ScalarTypeEnum) and pick the matching value field per
        // instance attribute. See the getObject docstring below for
        // the dataType → field mapping.
        const instAttrSel = includeInstanceAttributes
            ? `attributes {
                        id displayName relativeName dataType dataSource importance
                        boolValue intValue floatValue stringValue
                        datetimeValue
                        intervalValue { seconds minutes hours days months years }
                        objectValue
                        enumerationValue enumerationName
                        geopointValue referencedNodeId
                      }`
            : '';

        const instSel = includeInstances
            ? `objectsByTypeId${instFilter} {
                    id displayName relativeName description typeId typeName
                    ${instAttrSel}
                  }`
            : '';

        const subTypesSel = includeSubTypes
            ? `subTypes { id displayName relativeName }`
            : '';

        const superTypeSel = includeSuperType
            ? `subTypeOf { id displayName relativeName }`
            : '';

        // childEquipment is the SMIP "this type is composed of these
        // equipment subtypes" relation. Field shape on this build is
        // best-effort — if the schema exposes more fields (multiplicity,
        // equipmentType, etc.) extend the selection here.
        const compositionSel = includeComposition
            ? `childEquipment { id displayName relativeName }`
            : '';

        const query = `
            query GetTypes {
              tiqTypes${typeFilter} {
                id displayName relativeName description fqn idPath
                ${typeAttrSel}
                ${instSel}
                ${subTypesSel}
                ${superTypeSel}
                ${compositionSel}
              }
            }
        `;

        const resp = await tiqJSHelper.invokeGraphQLAsync(query);
        return (resp && resp.data && resp.data.tiqTypes) || [];
    },

    // Query — fetch a single object by id with optional related-data
    // subselections. The everyday workhorse for type-bound display
    // scripts that need to load their own instance.
    //
    // SCOPE: Static value fields only. DYNAMIC-resolved attributes
    // (dataSource = TAG / INTERNAL) won't be populated here — their
    // values live in time series storage and need a future TS-focused
    // fetcher (getTimeSeries / getRawHistoryDataWithSampling). Route
    // on attribute.dataSource to know which path to take.
    //
    // Args shape:
    //   {
    //     nodeId:              String  — digits-only SMIP object node id (REQUIRED).
    //     includeAttributes:   Boolean — default TRUE. Returns ALL TEN value-variant
    //                                    columns plus dataType on each attribute,
    //                                    so the caller can route on
    //                                    `attribute.dataType` (ScalarTypeEnum)
    //                                    without a second round-trip. Mapping:
    //
    //                                      attr.dataType    →  field on attr to read
    //                                      ───────────────     ──────────────────────
    //                                      "BOOL"           →  attr.boolValue          (Boolean)
    //                                      "INT"            →  attr.intValue           (BigInt — string in JSON)
    //                                      "FLOAT"          →  attr.floatValue         (Float)
    //                                      "STRING"         →  attr.stringValue        (String)
    //                                      "DATETIME"       →  attr.datetimeValue      (Datetime, ISO-8601)
    //                                      "INTERVAL"       →  attr.intervalValue      (Interval object —
    //                                                          { seconds, minutes, hours, days, months, years };
    //                                                          NOT the IntervalInput shape used on write)
    //                                      "OBJECT"         →  attr.objectValue        (JSON)
    //                                      "ENUMERATION"    →  attr.enumerationValue   (String — selected value)
    //                                                       +  attr.enumerationName    (String — human label)
    //                                      "GEOPOINT"       →  attr.geopointValue      (String — schema types it as String)
    //                                      "REFERENCE"      →  attr.referencedNodeId   (BigInt — string in JSON; FK to another node)
    //
    //                                    Empty value fields come back as null.
    //                                    relativeName and id/displayName are also returned
    //                                    on every attribute regardless of dataType.
    //
    //     includePartOf:       Boolean — default FALSE. Adds the parent object (id /
    //                                    displayName / typeId / typeName).
    //     includeChildObjects: Boolean — default FALSE. Adds direct children
    //                                    (id / displayName / description /
    //                                    typeId / typeName).
    //     includeType:         Boolean — default FALSE. Adds a small type pointer
    //                                    (id / displayName / relativeName).
    //   }
    //
    // Returns the object dict with whatever subselections are turned
    // on, or null if no object with that id was found. The shape is
    // intentionally not flattened — same shape as `Query.object` in
    // GraphQL with the optional fields tacked on.
    //
    // Use this in any type-bound display script that needs to load
    // its own node from `context.std_inputs.node_id`. For domain-specific
    // result shaping (e.g. flattening a PSD Result into row form),
    // use a tenant-specific SDK method on top of this generic fetch.
    getObject: async function(args){
        args = args || {};
        const nodeId                = String(args.nodeId || '').trim();
        const includeAttributes     = (args.includeAttributes !== false);  // default TRUE
        const includePartOf         = !!args.includePartOf;
        const includeChildObjects   = !!args.includeChildObjects;
        const includeChildAttributes= !!args.includeChildAttributes;
        const includeType           = !!args.includeType;

        if (!nodeId || !/^\d+$/.test(nodeId)) {
            throw new Error('getObject: nodeId must be digits-only; got ' + JSON.stringify(nodeId));
        }

        // Each subselection is its own conditional fragment, spliced in
        // only when the toggle is on — keeps the round-trip lean.
        //
        // The attributes fragment returns ALL TEN value-variant columns
        // plus dataType, so the caller can read attribute.dataType
        // (ScalarTypeEnum: BOOL / INT / FLOAT / STRING / DATETIME /
        // INTERVAL / OBJECT / ENUMERATION / GEOPOINT / REFERENCE) and
        // pick the matching value field — without a second round-trip
        // and without guessing. Empty value fields come back as null.
        // intervalValue is an Interval object on read (not the
        // IntervalInput shape used on write); BigInt fields (intValue,
        // referencedNodeId) come across as strings in JSON. The
        // enumerationName companion stays alongside enumerationValue
        // for human-readable rendering of ENUMERATION attributes.
        const attrsSel = includeAttributes
            ? `attributes {
                    id displayName relativeName dataType dataSource importance
                    boolValue intValue floatValue stringValue
                    datetimeValue
                    intervalValue { seconds minutes hours days months years }
                    objectValue
                    enumerationValue enumerationName
                    geopointValue referencedNodeId
                  }`
            : '';

        const partOfSel = includePartOf
            ? `partOf { id displayName relativeName typeId typeName }`
            : '';

        // The children's-own-attributes fragment is the same shape as the
        // parent's `attributes` block above. Folded inside `childObjects`
        // when both toggles are on, so the caller gets one round-trip
        // for "give me this object, its kids, and the kids' attribute
        // values" — which is the shape display scripts attached to a
        // parent type (e.g. a Busbar showing its Busplugs in a table)
        // tend to need.
        const childAttrsSel = (includeChildObjects && includeChildAttributes)
            ? `attributes {
                    id displayName relativeName dataType dataSource importance
                    boolValue intValue floatValue stringValue
                    datetimeValue
                    intervalValue { seconds minutes hours days months years }
                    objectValue
                    enumerationValue enumerationName
                    geopointValue referencedNodeId
                  }`
            : '';
        const childrenSel = includeChildObjects
            ? `childObjects {
                    id displayName relativeName description typeId typeName importance
                    ${childAttrsSel}
                  }`
            : '';

        const typeSel = includeType
            ? `type { id displayName relativeName }`
            : '';

        const query = `
            query GetObject {
              object(id: "${nodeId}") {
                id displayName relativeName description typeId typeName partOfId importance
                ${typeSel}
                ${attrsSel}
                ${partOfSel}
                ${childrenSel}
              }
            }
        `;

        const resp = await tiqJSHelper.invokeGraphQLAsync(query);
        return (resp && resp.data && resp.data.object) || null;
    },

    // Query — list enumerationTypes with optional substring filter on
    // displayName, and (by default) zip the four parallel arrays the
    // SMIP stores into one `enumerationTypeObjects` array of friendly
    // { name, value, color, description } objects per row. SMIP stores
    // each enum's four facets as four positionally-aligned arrays:
    //
    //   enumerationNames         = ["Alarm",   "Warning", "Info"]
    //   defaultEnumerationValues = ["1",       "2",       "3"]
    //   enumerationColorCodes    = ["#ff0000", "#ff8800", "#00aaff"]
    //   enumerationDescriptions  = ["Critical", "Warning", "Informational"]
    //
    // At index i, all four refer to the same enum value. The zip walks
    // i across the longest of the four and emits one object per slot.
    // If the SMIP has a sparse facet (e.g. no descriptions set yet)
    // the missing fields come back as null rather than undefined so
    // callers can do `if (obj.color)` without a typeof guard.
    //
    // Args shape (all optional):
    //   {
    //     displayNameSearch: String — case-insensitive substring on
    //                                 enumerationType.displayName. Empty = all.
    //     includeValues:     Boolean — default TRUE. When on, append
    //                                 enumerationTypeObjects to each row.
    //   }
    //
    // Returns the raw `enumerationTypes` array. Each entry is
    // `{ id, displayName, relativeName, enumerationNames,
    //    defaultEnumerationValues, enumerationColorCodes,
    //    enumerationDescriptions }` plus, when includeValues is on,
    // `enumerationTypeObjects: [{ name, value, color, description }, ...]`.
    getEnumTypes: async function(args){
        args = args || {};
        const displayNameSearch = String(args.displayNameSearch || '').trim();
        const includeValues     = (args.includeValues !== false);  // default TRUE

        // Same conditional-filter pattern as getTypes — omit the (filter:)
        // clause entirely when the search string is empty so the rendered
        // query stays as small as the caller asked for.
        const filter = displayNameSearch
            ? `(filter: { displayName: { includesInsensitive: ${JSON.stringify(displayNameSearch)} } })`
            : '';

        const query = `
            query GetEnumTypes {
              enumerationTypes${filter} {
                id displayName relativeName
                enumerationNames
                defaultEnumerationValues
                enumerationColorCodes
                enumerationDescriptions
              }
            }
        `;

        const resp = await tiqJSHelper.invokeGraphQLAsync(query);
        const rows = (resp && resp.data && resp.data.enumerationTypes) || [];

        if (!includeValues) return rows;

        for (const row of rows) {
            const names  = row.enumerationNames         || [];
            const values = row.defaultEnumerationValues || [];
            const colors = row.enumerationColorCodes    || [];
            const descs  = row.enumerationDescriptions  || [];
            const n = Math.max(names.length, values.length, colors.length, descs.length);
            const objs = [];
            for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                objs.push({
                    name:        i < names.length  ? names[i]  : null,
                    value:       i < values.length ? values[i] : null,
                    color:       i < colors.length ? colors[i] : null,
                    description: i < descs.length  ? descs[i]  : null,
                });
            }
            row.enumerationTypeObjects = objs;
        }
        return rows;
    },

});

</script>

<?php

use GuzzleHttp\Client;
use GuzzleHttp\Exception\GuzzleException;

class Guzzler
{
    // Generic Guzzle wrapper. The endpoint and api-key strings here are
    // sample values — re-target by editing the two private fields below
    // for the API you're integrating with. The header / URL-param key
    // patterns are kept as commented-out reminders; uncomment whichever
    // one matches your target API's auth model.
    private $apiKey       = "abc-***-***-123";
    private $api_endpoint = "https://example.com/api/v1/";
    private $headers      = array(
        "Content-Type" => "application/json",
        // use this in case the api key goes into the header
        // "apiKey" => $this->apiKey
    );

    private $client;

    public function __construct()
    {
        $this->client = new Client();
    }

    public function GetAsync($query, $getRawContent = false)
    {
        try{
            // use this in case the api key goes into the url parameters
            // if(str_contains($query, '?')){
            //     $query = $query . '&apiKey=' . $this->apiKey;
            // } else {
            //     $query = $query . '?apiKey=' . $this->apiKey;
            // }

            $response = $this->client->request('GET', $this->api_endpoint . $query, ['headers' => $this->headers]);
            $content = $response->getBody()->getContents();
            if($getRawContent){
                // use this to return text or xml
                return $content;
            } else {
                // use this for normal json
                $result = json_decode($content);
                return $result;
            }
        } catch (GuzzleException $e){
            return $e->getMessage();
        }
    }

    public function PutAsync($query, $body = null)
    {
        try{
            $response = $this->client->request('PUT', $this->api_endpoint . $query, ['headers' => $this->headers, 'body' => json_encode($body)]);
            $content = $response->getBody()->getContents();
            $result = json_decode($content);
            return $result;
        } catch (GuzzleException $e){
            return $e->getMessage();
        }
    }

    public function DeleteAsync($query)
    {
        try{
            $response = $this->client->request('DELETE', $this->api_endpoint . $query, ['headers' => $this->headers]);
            $content = $response->getBody()->getContents();
            $result = json_decode($content);
            return $result;
        } catch (GuzzleException $e){
            return $e->getMessage();
        }
    }

    public function PostAsync($query, $body = null)
    {
        try{
            $response = $this->client->request('POST', $this->api_endpoint . $query, ['headers' => $this->headers, 'body' => json_encode($body)]);
            $content = $response->getBody()->getContents();
            $result = json_decode($content);
            return $result;
        } catch (GuzzleException $e){
            return $e->getMessage();
        }
    }

    public function PatchAsync($query, $body = null)
    {
        try{
            $response = $this->client->request('PATCH', $this->api_endpoint . $query, ['headers' => $this->headers, 'body' => json_encode($body)]);
            $content = $response->getBody()->getContents();
            $result = json_decode($content);
            return $result;
        } catch (GuzzleException $e){
            return $e->getMessage();
        }
    }
}
